_comment: Maps QAFlywheel failure modes to (a) human-readable description, (b) the routing layer's recommended response when this mode dominates a (artifact, difficulty, category) bucket. The Mac app's Picker can read this at goal-classification time: classify the goal → bucket → look up dominant failure mode in success-rates.json → consult this taxonomy for action. Future Phase: Swift integration in OllamaCapabilityPicker.shouldEscalateOrSimplify(goal:). For now, this is documentation + Cloud Function consumption.

success.description: The wizard produced a parseable artifact that satisfies the catalog entry's success criteria (capabilities found).

wizard-budget-exhausted.description: QAFlywheel script gave up at its 14-min budget (default). Wizard was still grinding through StepwiseRetry attempts. Common when each retry takes 2-3 min and budget=4: total=12 min, plus planner+verifier overhead → fits, but adds JSON-mode planner+verifier round-trips and the budget elapses. Indicates the model isn't converging on this goal complexity within the budget; either the goal is too big for this size class, the prompt is too complex for the model to follow on retry, or both.
_actions: Try gpt-oss:20b instead of qwen3-coder:30b (better instruction-following on multi-stage prompts)
Reduce decomposeRetryBudget for this bucket (each attempt takes 2-3 min; 4 attempts may exceed 14-min wall budget)
Lower reasoning level / output token cap so generator finishes faster
If catalog entry's expected capabilities are too rich for the size class, downgrade prompt OR upgrade to a bigger model tier

stuck-step-no-resolution.description: Wizard hit StepwiseRetry decomposeRetryBudget OR diminishing-returns threshold, surfaced stuck-step-pause to the user (or auto-picked endSession in headless mode). The model literally couldn't produce parseable output across the retry attempts.
_actions: Switch to a different Ollama family (qwen3-coder:30b → gpt-oss:20b — different training data may break the boilerplate fixation)
Increase artifact-spec rigor in the planner prompt (concrete in-domain example matters)
If bucket's ok rate is <30%, route the wizard's main system prompt at this category to a heavier reasoning model

wizard-error-other.description: Wizard returned a session in 'failed' phase for reasons other than StepwiseRetry exhaustion — Ollama daemon unreachable, parse-and-recover failed, dependency error, etc.
_actions: If recurring, file a bug — this is unexpected

skipped-dry-run.description: Catalog entry was scheduled in dry-run mode (no actual wizard call). Reserved for catalog validation.

unknown.description: Status field couldn't be classified — probably a script regression or new outcome state. Investigate.

_routingHints._comment: When the picker classifies a new user goal into a bucket and looks up success-rates.json for that bucket, these hints apply. Future routing layer can consume directly via MchataisourceCacheService.
_routingHints.highOkRate>=0.7: Use Lowest Cost defaults (qwen3-coder:30b for coding, gpt-oss:20b for planning). Budget=4 retry attempts.
_routingHints.moderateOkRate>=0.3: Bias toward gpt-oss:20b even for coding tasks (better instruction-following). Consider increasing decomposeRetryBudget to 6 if user is willing to wait. Surface a 'this prompt is in a hard category — generation may take 10-15 min' hint to the user.
_routingHints.lowOkRate<0.3: Auto-route to Best Quality mode (Claude Sonnet 4.6 via Anthropic API) UNLESS user explicitly opted into Lowest Cost. If Lowest Cost forced, surface a 'this prompt class has historically failed in Lowest Cost mode' warning + offer to escalate. Or suggest the user simplify the prompt.
_routingHints.noSamples: Brand-new bucket — use Lowest Cost defaults but log the run with extra diagnostics for the next aggregator pass.

_keyEmpiricalFinding20260430._comment: Multi-canary observation 2026-04-30: with maxTurns=1, first-turn baseline success rate on miniApp L1 productivity AND L1 games is ~0% — qwen3-coder:30b consistently hits stuck-step-no-resolution within the StepwiseRetry budget. BUT Lawrence's manual Kitchen Timer succeeded on the user-facing flow (maxTurns=2) at retry attempt 4 of turn 2. The 'auto-steer turn 2' path is where convergence actually happens.
_keyEmpiricalFinding20260430.implication: Per-model success curves from QAFlywheel runs are FIRST-TURN BASELINE, not user-facing-flow performance. The user-facing flow with maxTurns=2 has a higher effective success rate because it gets a second chance after stuck-pause. Don't compare canary numbers to user-experience numbers directly.
_keyEmpiricalFinding20260430._actionForRouting: When a goal classifies into a low-first-turn-baseline bucket but the user is on the production path (maxTurns=2 default), DON'T auto-route to Best Quality — give the auto-steer turn 2 path a chance first. Only escalate if turn 2 also fails.
_keyEmpiricalFinding20260430._actionForFlywheel: QAFlywheel canaries should report TWO success rates: first-turn-baseline (maxTurns=1) and full-flow (maxTurns=2). The latter matches user experience; the former is faster to evaluate.
